question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a network identifier that causes StringUtils.isNotBlank(bucketName) to return false and getBlockStreamBucketName() to resolve to an attacker-controlled or nonexistent bucket name, causing all importer instances relying on that network to fail block downloads and halt processing on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction or state change that corrupts the importerProperties.getNetwork() return value, causing getBucketName() to derive an incorrect bucket name and causing block file downloads to fail across all importer instances sharing that network config, shutting down 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fallback logic in getBucketName() — where a blank bucketName causes a network-derived name to be used — by poisoning the network name in the shared configuration store (e.g., environment variable or config map), redirecting all importer nodes to a nonexistent bucket and halting block ingestion on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: autoDiscoveryEnabled)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malicious registered block node into the database (via an unauthenticated API endpoint or a publicly writable table) such that autoDiscoveryEnabled=true causes BlockNodeDiscoveryService to discover and use the attacker's node, which then returns malformed or empty block streams, causing 30%+ of importer nodes to fail block processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: autoDiscoveryEnabled)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated RegisteredNodeChangedEvents (by creating/deleting registered nodes via a public API) to continuously invalidate the BlockNodeDiscoveryService cache, causing repeated expensive DB queries and resource exhaustion that degrades or halts block processing on 30%+ of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: nodes)] Can an unprivileged external user, by injecting a large number of malicious BlockNodeProperties entries into the nodes collection via a publicly accessible configuration endpoint, cause the importer to exhaust connection pool resources attempting to connect to unreachable hosts, halting block processing on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: nodes)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a BlockNodeProperties entry with a host pointing to a slow/tarpit server such that the importer's connection attempts block indefinitely, consuming all available threads and halting block ingestion on 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: sourceType)] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the sourceType property (e.g., via an exposed actuator or config endpoint) to BlockSourceType.BLOCK_NODE when no valid block nodes are available, causing CompositeBlockSource to always attempt block node streaming and fail, halting block processing on 30%+ of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: sourceType)] Can an unprivileged external user force sourceType to BlockSourceType.FILE when cloud storage is unavailable or misconfigured, causing all importer instances to fail block file downloads and halt processing on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: frequency)] Can an unprivileged external user set the frequency field to Duration.ofMillis(0) or a near-zero value (bypassing the @NotNull constraint but not a minimum constraint) to cause CompositeBlockSource's @Scheduled task to spin at maximum rate, exhausting CPU and thread resources and halting effective block processing on 30%+ of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: frequency)] Can an unprivileged external user set the frequency field to an extremely large Duration value, causing the @Scheduled polling in CompositeBlockSource to effectively stop executing, halting block ingestion on 30%+ of importer nodes without any brute force?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user flip the enabled flag from true to false via an exposed Spring Boot actuator /env endpoint, causing the block importer to stop processing blocks entirely on all importer instances, shutting down 30%+ of network processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: ledger)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted on-chain transaction that corrupts the LedgerProperties.historyProofVerificationKey stored in the database, causing toLedger() to produce an invalid Ledger object that fails TSS verification for every block, halting block processing on 30%+ of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: ledger)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that causes the LedgerProperties.ledgerId byte array to be set to an empty or null value, bypassing the @NotEmpty constraint at runtime and causing NullPointerException or verification failure in toLedger(), halting block processing on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: ledger)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malformed nodeContributions list into LedgerProperties (e.g., via a public API that writes to the registered node table) such that toLedger() produces a Ledger with invalid contributions, causing block verification to fail on all importer instances and halting processing on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: persistBytes)] Can an unprivileged external user flip persistBytes to true via an exposed configuration endpoint, causing all importer instances to write large block byte arrays to the database, exhausting disk or database storage and halting block processing on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: writeFiles)] Can an unprivileged external user flip writeFiles to true via an exposed configuration endpoint, causing all importer instances to write verified block stream files to the filesystem, exhausting disk space and halting block processing on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: stream)] Can an unprivileged external user set StreamProperties.maxBlockItems to the minimum allowed value of 1000 (via an exposed config endpoint), causing the importer to reject large blocks that exceed this limit and halt block processing on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: stream)] Can an unprivileged external user set StreamProperties.maxStreamResponseSize to a very small value (e.g., 1 byte), causing all block node stream responses to be rejected as oversized and halting block processing on 30%+ of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: stream)] Can an unprivileged external user set StreamProperties.maxSubscribeAttempts to 1 (minimum allowed), causing the importer to mark block nodes as inactive after a single failed subscribe attempt, exhausting all available nodes and halting block processing on 30%+ of importer instances?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: BlockProperties (field: nodes)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a BlockNodeProperties entry with host set to a loopback address (127.0.0.1) and a port that is not listening, causing the importer to repeatedly fail connection attempts and exhaust the maxSubscribeAttempts threshold, marking all nodes inactive and halting block processing on 30%+ of nodes?
